title: Logical error: 'dict_structure.hasAttribute(attr_col_name) && "Attribute not found in dictionary structure of dictionary"'
description:
_Important: This issue was automatically generated and is used by CI for matching failures. DO NOT modify the body content. DO NOT remove labels._
Test name: Logical error: 'dict_structure.hasAttribute(attr_col_name) && "Attribute not found in dictionary structure of dictionary"'
CI report: [BuzzHouse (amd_ubsan)](https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=b151588d4706d3a6ca2449b81ff0a55471761e31&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=BuzzHouse%20%28amd_ubsan%29)
CIDB statistics: [cidb](https://play.clickhouse.com/play?user=play&run=1#)
### Describe the bug
Yes, I need a break. Only happens with the new analyzer enabled. Sorry @nihalzp
### How to reproduce
On a debug build run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Nullable(Int)) ENGINE = MergeTree() PRIMARY KEY tuple();
CREATE DICTIONARY d0 (c0 Nullable(Int) DEFAULT 1) PRIMARY KEY (c0) SOURCE(CLICKHOUSE(DB 'default' TABLE 't0')) LAYOUT(FLAT()) LIFETIME(1);
SELECT 1 FROM t0 WHERE dictGetInt32(d0, 'c0.null', c0) = 1;
```

key_bug_features: New analyzer enabled causes attribute not found error
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id Int32, name String, age Nullable(Int32)) ENGINE = MergeTree() PRIMARY KEY id;
CREATE DICTIONARY d1 (id Int32, age Nullable(Int32) DEFAULT 0) PRIMARY KEY (id) SOURCE(CLICKHOUSE(DB 'default' TABLE 't1')) LAYOUT(FLAT()) LIFETIME(1);
SELECT name FROM t1 WHERE dictGetInt32(d1, 'age', id) > 20;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t1 WHERE age IS NULL OR dictGetInt32(d1, 'age', id) = 0;
